` type EmailData struct { UserName string Subject string FeedbackContent string } func BuildFeedbackEmail(fromAddr string, toAddr string, userName string, subject string, feedbackContent string) (emailBody string, err error) { // Validate input parameters if fromAddr == "" || toAddr == "" { return "", fmt.Errorf("fromAddr and toAddr cannot be empty") } if userName == "" || subject == "" || feedbackContent == "" { return "", fmt.Errorf("userName, subject, and feedbackContent cannot be empty") } // Create template data data := EmailData{ UserName: userName, Subject: subject, FeedbackContent: feedbackContent, } // Parse and execute template tmpl, err := template.New("emailTemplate").Parse(emailTemplate) if err != nil { return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to parse template: %w", err) } var buf bytes.Buffer err = tmpl.Execute(&buf, data) if err != nil { return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to execute template: %w", err) } return buf.String(), nil } // Example of how to send the email (not part of the required function) func SendFeedbackEmail(auth smtp.Auth, fromAddr string, toAddr string, subject string, emailBody string) error { msg := fmt.Sprintf("To: %s\r\nSubject: %s\r\nContent-Type: text/html; charset=UTF-8\r\n\r\n%s", toAddr, subject, emailBody) err := smtp.SendMail("smtp.example.com:25", auth, fromAddr, []string{toAddr}, []byte(msg)) if err != nil { return fmt.Errorf("failed to send email: %w", err) } return nil } func main() { // Example usage fromAddr := "user@example.com" toAddr := "support@company.com" userName := "John Doe" subject := "Great product!" feedbackContent := "I love your product! It's exactly what I was looking for. The user interface is intuitive and the performance is excellent. Keep up the great work!" emailBody, err := BuildFeedbackEmail(fromAddr, toAddr, userName, subject, feedbackContent) if err != nil { fmt.Printf("Error building email: %v\n", err) return } fmt.Println("Email body generated successfully!") fmt.Println("Email content:") fmt.Println(emailBody) } ```

package main import ( "fmt" "html" ) // BuildFeedbackEmail generates an HTML-formatted email body for user feedback func BuildFeedbackEmail(fromAddr string, toAddr string, userName string, subject string, feedbackContent string) (emailBody string, err error) { // Escape user input to prevent HTML injection and ensure proper formatting escapedUserName := html.EscapeString(userName) escapedSubject := html.EscapeString(subject) escapedFeedbackContent := html.EscapeString(feedbackContent) // Build HTML email body with inline styles for better email client compatibility emailBody = fmt.Sprintf(` User Feedback

User Feedback Report

From: %s
To: %s
User Name: %s
Feedback Subject: %s
Feedback Content:
%s
`, html.EscapeString(fromAddr), html.EscapeString(toAddr), escapedUserName, escapedSubject, escapedFeedbackContent) return emailBody, nil } // Example usage and testing func main() { // Test the function emailBody, err := BuildFeedbackEmail( "user@example.com", "support@company.com", "John Doe", "Feature Request: Dark Mode", "I would love to see a dark mode option in the application.\n\nCurrently, the bright interface causes eye strain during night usage.\n\nThank you for considering this feature!", ) if err != nil { panic(err) } fmt.Println("Generated Email Body:") fmt.Println(emailBody) }
